message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 182 update_execution_step cx-threaded across 4 step-loop call sites (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Fifth tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig. Migrates the
per-step progress-tracking write — called 4x on the normal
execution path.

New helpers on WorkflowRunner:
- `update_execution_step_with_cx` (leaf) — routes both the
  read (get_workflow_with_cx) and write (upsert_workflow_with_cx)
  through storage cx-first siblings. The "externally modified"
  status check stays inline (pure enum match on already-fetched
  record; no cx threading needed).
- `update_execution_step_maybe_cx` (dispatcher) — takes
  `Option<&Cx>`; when `Some` calls `_with_cx`, otherwise falls
  through to the legacy helper. Keeps the 4 call sites single-
  line rather than match-on-cx boilerplate at each.

Call-site migrations in `run_workflow_inner`'s step loop:
- L885 (post-step-success progress update)
- L937 (post-wait-condition progress update)
- L1210 (post-retry progress update)
- L1343 (post-send progress update)

All four now route through `update_execution_step_maybe_cx(cx, ...)`.
When `run_workflow_with_cx` is the caller, `cx: Some(...)` flows
all the way into storage. When legacy `run_workflow` is the
caller (cx: None), behavior is byte-for-byte identical — the
dispatcher's `if let Some(cx) = cx` short-circuits to the legacy
helper.

Legacy `update_execution_step` (L1924) unchanged. It's still the
leaf for ambient-cx callers and for compatibility with the 5th
remaining call site (at L1971, inside the dispatcher).

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass.

Strangler-fig cancellation coverage of `run_workflow_with_cx`:
- Pre-flight checkpoint (tick 178)
- Startup storage reads (tick 180, 4 sites)
- Per-step loop checkpoint + cleanup on cancel (tick 181)
- Per-step progress write (tick 182, 4 sites — this)

Not yet cx-threaded (future ticks):
- fail_execution, complete_execution, mark_trigger_event_handled,
  set_execution_waiting — lifecycle helpers on error/completion
  paths
- Internal `check_step_idempotency`, `record_workflow_terminal_action`,
  and step `.execute(&mut ctx).await` invocations
- Two legacy callers of run_workflow (tick 183)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
